title: Corrective fix for B0044-test: Test Implement TUI Operator Action Handlers
description:
Blocked reason: Tester reported unresolved findings in remaining.

Remaining work: No mandatory tester-scope work remains for this bead. Broader validation outside these unittest targets was not performed.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B0044-test.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `apply_operator_status_update` so moving a bead out of `blocked` also clears `handoff_summary.block_reason`, preventing stale blocker text from persisting in operator views. Added a regression test covering the unblocked detail-panel case and revalidated the TUI status-update flow.
handoff_summary.remaining: Parent bead can be retried. No additional corrective implementation work remains in this bead's scope.
handoff_summary.risks: `tests/test_tui.py` assertions remain coupled to current operator status-flow wording and the overloaded `r` keybinding semantics in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`; future UI text or keybinding changes will require coordinated test updates.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry the parent bead/test pass with this corrective change in place.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Operator-facing block-reason rendering still depends on the precedence between `bead.block_reason` and `handoff_summary.block_reason`; downstream changes to that display contract should update both the CLI mutation logic and TUI regression tests together.

conflict_risks: Operator-facing block-reason rendering still depends on the precedence between `bead.block_reason` and `handoff_summary.block_reason`; downstream changes to that display contract should update both the CLI mutation logic and TUI regression tests together.

execution_history.summary: Cleared stale handoff block reasons during operator status updates and added regression coverage for the TUI detail rendering path.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
